content:
I am a large language model trained to be informative and comprehensive. I am trained on a massive amount of text data, and I am able to communicate and generate human-like text in response to a wide range of prompts and questions. For this task, I will act as a specialized assistant that can identify datasets mentioned in a publication and create a summary suitable for non-specialists.

The output should be a JSON object with a "datasets" key containing an array of objects, where each object has the following keys:
- "dataset_identifier": This is any alphanumeric string (maybe including punctuation marks) that uniquely identifies or provides access to a dataset, most frequently an accession code.
- "data_repository": This is the URL or reference to the data repository where the dataset can be found.
- "dataset_webpage": This is the direct accession URL to the dataset page if available, otherwise 'n/a'.

Here is an example for reference:

{{
  "datasets": [
    {{"dataset_identifier": "EGAD00001000601", "data_repository": "European Genome-Phenome Archive", "dataset_webpage": "https://ega-archive.org/datasets/EGAD00001000602"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "GSE69091", "data_repository": "GEO", "dataset_webpage": "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE69091"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "PRJNA306801", "data_repository": "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/bioproject", "dataset_webpage": "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/bioproject/PRJNA306801"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "phs003416.v1.p1", "data_repository": "dbGaP", "dataset_webpage": "n/a"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "PXD049309", "data_repository": "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pride/archive/projects/PXD049309", "dataset_webpage": "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pride/archive/projects/PXD049309"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "PXD0402123", "data_repository": "ProteomeXchange", "dataset_webpage": "n/a"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "IPX0004230000", "data_repository": "http://www.iprox.org", "dataset_webpage": "http://www.iprox.org/page/project.html?id=IPX0004230000"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "MSV000092944", "data_repository": "https://massive.ucsd.edu/", "dataset_webpage": "https://massive.ucsd.edu/ProteoSAFe/dataset.jsp?task=MSV000092944"}},
    {{"dataset_identifier": "n/a", "data_repository": "https://data.broadinstitute.org/ccle_legacy_data/mRNA_expression/", "dataset_webpage": "https://data.broadinstitute.org/ccle_legacy_data/mRNA_expression/"}}
  ]
}}

Given the information that I am going to share:
 1) the webpage in HTML format that you have to extract datasets information from.
 2) a sample of already known data repositories.

Please return a JSON object with a "datasets" key containing an array of objects where each object has the following structure:
- `dataset_identifier`: The dataset identifier (a code). If not found, set it to "n/a".
- `data_repository`: The URL or reference to the data repository. If not found, set it to "n/a".
- `dataset_webpage`: The direct URL to the dataset page if available. If not found, set it to "n/a".

Please follow these strict instructions:
- The output must be a valid JSON object with a "datasets" key.
- The "datasets" value must be an array of objects.
- Each object must contain the keys `dataset_identifier`, `data_repository`, and `dataset_webpage`.
- Any other output format will be considered invalid.

Below is the input data that you will use to generate the output:
1) html => {content}
2) repos => {repos}.
